articles.headline: India Unveils 'Green Hydrogen for Bharat' Phase II: Boosting Domestic Production and Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, branded 'Green Hydrogen for Bharat', with enhanced incentives for production and infrastructure development.
• The new phase focuses on scaling up green hydrogen production capacity to 10 million metric tonnes per annum (MMTPA) by 2030 and attracting over $100 billion in investments.
• Key components include dedicated manufacturing zones, R&D funding for advanced electrolyser technologies, and export promotion strategies for green hydrogen and its derivatives.
• The mission aims to significantly reduce India's reliance on fossil fuel imports, create 600,000 jobs, and contribute to achieving net-zero emissions by 2070.
• Subsidies and viability gap funding will be provided for early adopters in hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and heavy-duty transport.
gsSubTopics: Energy
Infrastructure
Environmental pollution & degradation
Government policies & interventions
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Phase II target: 10 MMTPA green hydrogen production by 2030.
Expected investment: Over $100 billion.
Job creation target: 600,000.
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy.
Key components: SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme, R&D initiatives.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's energy security and geopolitical implications of green hydrogen leadership.
Analyze the economic potential and challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and export.
Evaluate the role of government incentives and private sector participation in achieving mission targets.
Examine the environmental benefits and contribution to India's climate commitments.
Critique the technological hurdles and infrastructure requirements for widespread adoption.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production plant with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all normal matter.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces Right to Privacy, Issues Guidelines on Digital Surveillance
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, reiterated the inviolability of the Right to Privacy under Article 21, asserting that arbitrary digital surveillance by the state is unconstitutional.
• The judgment established a stringent three-part test for any state surveillance: legality, necessity, and proportionality, with judicial oversight as a mandatory component.
• It mandated the establishment of an independent oversight mechanism, possibly involving retired judges or technical experts, to review and approve surveillance requests.
• The court also directed the government to frame a comprehensive data protection law that specifically addresses state access to personal data and ensures accountability.
• This ruling is seen as a significant step towards balancing national security concerns with individual civil liberties in the digital age.
gsSubTopics: Fundamental Rights
Judiciary
Government policies & interventions
Information Technology
prelimsPoints: Right to Privacy is part of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty).
K.S. Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared privacy a fundamental right.
Three-part test for surveillance: legality, necessity, proportionality.
Mandatory judicial oversight for surveillance requests.
Directive to frame comprehensive data protection law.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving interpretation of the Right to Privacy in the digital era.
Analyze the challenges in balancing national security with individual civil liberties in surveillance policies.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in upholding fundamental rights against state overreach.
Suggest reforms required in India's legal framework for digital surveillance and data protection.
Examine the international best practices in surveillance oversight and data governance.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on a law book with a blurred background of binary code, symbolizing legal decisions in the digital realm.
articles.trivia: The 'reasonable expectation of privacy' test originated in the US Supreme Court case Katz v. United States (1967).

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Nears Conclusion, Poised to Reshape Bilateral Trade
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the ambitious India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) are reportedly in their final stages, with both sides expressing optimism for an imminent signing.
• The agreement aims to significantly reduce tariffs on a wide range of goods and services, potentially boosting bilateral trade by over 40% in the next five years.
• Key sectors expected to benefit include automobiles, textiles, pharmaceuticals, agricultural products, and digital services, with provisions for intellectual property rights and sustainable development.
• While sensitive issues like market access for specific agricultural products and data localization remain, negotiators are confident of finding common ground.
• This FTA is strategically important for India to diversify its trade partnerships and enhance its position in global supply chains, while offering the EU access to a vast and growing market.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
India and its neighbourhood- relations
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: FTA aims to boost India-EU bilateral trade by over 40%.
Key sectors: automobiles, textiles, pharmaceuticals, agriculture, digital services.
EU is India's third-largest trading partner.
FTA includes provisions on IPR and sustainable development.
Negotiations started in 2007, resumed in 2021 after a hiatus.
mainsPoints: Analyze the economic implications of the India-EU FTA for various sectors in India.
Discuss the strategic significance of this FTA in the context of India's 'Act East' policy and global trade diversification.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for Indian businesses arising from increased competition and market access in the EU.
Examine how the FTA addresses modern trade concerns like environmental standards, labor rights, and digital trade.
Critique the role of FTAs in shaping global economic order and India's position within it.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union superimposed over a graph showing upward economic trends.
articles.trivia: The EU is a unique economic and political union of 27 member states that are located primarily in Europe.

articles.headline: BRICS Summit 2025 Concludes with Focus on Multilateralism and Global South Development
articles.explainer:
• The annual BRICS Summit, held in Cape Town, South Africa, concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing strengthening multilateralism and promoting the interests of the Global South.
• Member nations reiterated their commitment to reforming global governance institutions, including the UN Security Council, and ensuring greater representation for developing countries.
• Discussions focused on enhancing intra-BRICS trade and investment, exploring mechanisms for de-dollarization, and strengthening cooperation in areas like climate change, health, and digital transformation.
• New members, admitted in the previous summit, actively participated, contributing to discussions on expanding the bloc's influence and fostering inclusive growth.
• The summit underscored BRICS's growing role as a counterweight to traditional Western-dominated forums and a voice for emerging economies.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora—their structure, mandate
India’s interests in global groupings
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: BRICS member countries (original and expanded).
Host city of the 2025 summit: Cape Town, South Africa.
Key agenda points: multilateralism, Global South, de-dollarization.
New Development Bank (NDB) is a BRICS initiative.
BRICS aims to reform global governance institutions.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving role of BRICS in the contemporary multipolar world order.
Discuss India's strategic interests and contributions within the BRICS framework.
Evaluate the feasibility and implications of de-dollarization efforts by BRICS nations.
Examine the challenges and opportunities presented by BRICS expansion for its cohesion and effectiveness.
Critique the potential impact of BRICS on global economic and political power dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders of BRICS nations shaking hands at the summit venue in Cape Town, South Africa.
articles.trivia: The acronym BRIC was coined in 2001 by Goldman Sachs economist Jim O'Neill to describe the four emerging economies of Brazil, Russia, India, and China.

articles.headline: Conservationists Raise Alarm as New Report Highlights Threats to Western Ghats Biodiversity
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) warns of escalating threats to the unique biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Key concerns include habitat fragmentation due to infrastructure projects, illegal mining, deforestation, and the increasing impact of climate change-induced extreme weather events.
• The report emphasizes the critical role of the Western Ghats as a global biodiversity hotspot and a source of numerous rivers, impacting the ecology and economy of peninsular India.
• It calls for stricter enforcement of environmental regulations, enhanced trans-state cooperation, and greater involvement of local communities in conservation efforts.
• Recommendations include establishing eco-sensitive zones around protected areas and promoting sustainable livelihoods to reduce human-wildlife conflict.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena
Environmental pollution & degradation
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats are one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Report by IISc (Indian Institute of Science).
Major threats: habitat fragmentation, illegal mining, deforestation, climate change.
Home to numerous endemic species of flora and fauna.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces.
Analyze the socio-economic implications of environmental degradation in the region.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current conservation policies and suggest measures for improvement.
Examine the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in biodiversity conservation.
Critique the balance between developmental projects and ecological preservation in ecologically sensitive areas.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with a waterfall, symbolizing its rich biodiversity.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and influence the Indian monsoon weather patterns.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Test Flight for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV)
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully conducting a crucial test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• The sub-orbital flight involved a controlled descent and autonomous landing, demonstrating key technologies for future low-cost access to space.
• This test is a step towards developing a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch vehicle, which aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches.
• The RLV program is crucial for India's ambitions in space commercialization, satellite deployment, and future human spaceflight missions (Gaganyaan).
• Further tests are planned to validate aerodynamic characteristics, thermal protection systems, and autonomous navigation for a full-scale reusable orbital vehicle.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Space technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD program aims for low-cost space access.
The test involved controlled descent and autonomous landing.
RLV is a step towards a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) vehicle.
Crucial for future space commercialization and Gaganyaan mission.
India is among few nations developing RLV technology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program and its global competitiveness.
Analyze the economic benefits of reducing launch costs for satellite deployment and space exploration.
Evaluate the technological challenges involved in developing fully reusable launch systems.
Examine the role of ISRO in fostering indigenous technological capabilities and promoting 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in space.
Critique the strategic implications of advanced space capabilities for national security and international relations.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) during re-entry into Earth's atmosphere.
articles.trivia: The first successful orbital reusable spacecraft was the Space Shuttle, developed by NASA, which had its maiden flight in 1981.

articles.headline: New National Cyber Security Framework 2.0 Unveiled to Safeguard Critical Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched the National Cyber Security Framework 2.0, focusing on enhanced protection for critical information infrastructure.
• The updated framework emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach, promoting greater collaboration between government agencies, private sector entities, and academic institutions.
• Key features include a robust incident response mechanism, mandatory security audits for critical sectors, and a national-level cyber threat intelligence sharing platform.
• It also introduces stricter compliance norms for data centers and cloud service providers, along with initiatives for skill development in cybersecurity.
• The framework aims to bolster India's resilience against sophisticated cyber threats and ensure the security of its digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Cyber security
Internal security challenges
Information Technology
prelimsPoints: Launched by MeitY (Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology).
Focus on Critical Information Infrastructure (CII).
Emphasizes multi-stakeholder collaboration.
Key features: incident response, security audits, threat intelligence sharing.
CERT-In is the national agency for cyber security incidents.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats and their implications for India's national security and economy.
Analyze the strengths and weaknesses of India's current cybersecurity posture and the need for Framework 2.0.
Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships in building a resilient cybersecurity ecosystem.
Examine the challenges in implementing a comprehensive cyber security framework across diverse sectors.
Critique the balance between state surveillance for security and safeguarding individual data privacy in the digital age.
articles.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon superimposed on a map of India, representing national cybersecurity.
articles.trivia: The first National Cyber Security Policy of India was released in 2013.

articles.headline: Mid-Term Review of NEP 2020 Implementation Reveals Mixed Progress and Key Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a mid-term review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting both achievements and persistent challenges.
• Progress has been noted in foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) initiatives, curriculum reforms, and the integration of technology in education.
• However, significant hurdles remain in achieving the stipulated Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) targets in higher education, addressing teacher shortages, and ensuring equitable access in remote areas.
• The report calls for accelerated efforts in teacher training, infrastructure development, and greater collaboration between central and state governments for effective policy rollout.
• Stakeholders emphasized the need for flexible implementation strategies tailored to regional specificities and robust funding mechanisms to realize NEP's transformative vision.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry
Social empowerment
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
Focus areas: FLN, curriculum flexibility, multidisciplinary approach.
Challenges: GER targets, teacher shortages, infrastructure.
NIPUN Bharat Mission is a key FLN initiative.
3-language formula is a part of NEP recommendations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformational potential of NEP 2020 for India's education system.
Discuss the key successes and challenges faced during the mid-term implementation of NEP 2020.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current strategies in addressing issues of equity, access, and quality in education.
Suggest policy interventions and resource mobilization strategies to overcome implementation hurdles.
Examine the role of technology and teacher training in achieving the goals of NEP 2020.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom, with a teacher engaging them through digital tools, symbolizing education reform.
articles.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's education policy, advocating for a common school system.

articles.headline: Government Expands PLI Scheme to Boost Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) Battery Manufacturing
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced an expansion of the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme, specifically targeting Advanced Chemistry Cell (ACC) battery manufacturing in India.
• This move aims to establish a robust domestic supply chain for electric vehicles (EVs) and renewable energy storage, reducing reliance on imports.
• The expanded scheme offers enhanced incentives for companies investing in Giga-factories for ACC battery production, covering a wider range of battery technologies.
• It is expected to attract significant foreign direct investment (FDI) and foster indigenous research and development in battery technology.
• The initiative aligns with India's climate goals and 'Make in India' vision, positioning the country as a global hub for green technology manufacturing.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Science and Technology - developments and their applications
prelimsPoints: PLI scheme expanded for ACC battery manufacturing.
Aims to reduce import dependence for EVs and energy storage.
Incentives for Giga-factories.
Aligned with 'Make in India' and climate goals.
Different types of advanced chemistry cells include Lithium-ion, Solid-state batteries.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of ACC battery manufacturing for India's energy transition and EV ecosystem.
Analyze the impact of the PLI scheme on attracting investment and fostering indigenous capabilities in green technology.
Evaluate the challenges in establishing a competitive domestic battery manufacturing industry.
Examine the economic and environmental benefits of localizing battery production.
Critique the role of government incentives in driving industrial growth and technological innovation.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing various types of battery cells with the 'Make in India' logo.
articles.trivia: The global demand for lithium-ion batteries is projected to grow significantly, driven primarily by the electric vehicle market.

articles.headline: Indian Navy Concludes Major Multilateral Exercise in Indo-Pacific, Enhancing Maritime Security
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy successfully concluded a large-scale multilateral naval exercise, 'Malabar Plus', in the Indo-Pacific region, involving navies from key partner nations.
• The exercise focused on interoperability, anti-submarine warfare, maritime surveillance, and humanitarian assistance/disaster relief (HADR) operations.
• Participating nations included members of the Quad, alongside other regional partners, underscoring a collective commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
• The drills aimed at enhancing maritime domain awareness and strengthening cooperation amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics and increasing challenges to rules-based international order.
• This exercise reaffirms India's growing role as a net security provider and a crucial partner in maintaining stability in the strategically vital Indo-Pacific.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighbourhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Security challenges and their management in border areas
prelimsPoints: Exercise 'Malabar Plus' conducted in Indo-Pacific.
Involved Quad members and other regional partners.
Focus on anti-submarine warfare, maritime surveillance, HADR.
Aims to enhance interoperability and regional stability.
India is a key player in the Indo-Pacific strategy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the Indo-Pacific region for India and its partners.
Analyze how multilateral naval exercises like 'Malabar Plus' contribute to regional security and stability.
Evaluate the role of India as a net security provider in the Indo-Pacific.
Examine the challenges posed by geopolitical rivalries and maritime disputes in the region.
Critique the effectiveness of such drills in deterring aggressive actions and upholding international law.
articles.imageDescription: Warships from multiple navies sailing in formation during a joint exercise on the open sea.
articles.trivia: The original Malabar exercise started in 1992 as a bilateral exercise between the Indian and US navies.

articles.headline: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs Launches National Strategy to Combat Urban Heat Island Effect
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveiled a comprehensive National Strategy to mitigate the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in major Indian cities.
• The strategy focuses on promoting green infrastructure, cool roofs, permeable pavements, and urban forestry to reduce ambient temperatures in densely populated areas.
• It mandates urban local bodies to integrate UHI mitigation measures into their master plans and building codes, with incentives for green building practices.
• Pilot projects will be initiated in 10 major cities to develop localized action plans and assess their effectiveness in reducing heat stress and energy consumption.
• The initiative aims to enhance urban resilience to climate change, improve public health outcomes, and reduce energy demand for cooling.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Environmental pollution & degradation
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: UHI effect refers to warmer temperatures in urban areas compared to surrounding rural areas.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the strategy.
Measures include green roofs, cool pavements, urban forests.
Aims to reduce heat stress and energy consumption.
Pilot projects in 10 major cities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of the Urban Heat Island effect on Indian cities and their inhabitants.
Analyze the effectiveness of various green infrastructure and urban planning interventions in mitigating UHI.
Evaluate the challenges faced by urban local bodies in implementing sustainable and climate-resilient urban development strategies.
Examine the co-benefits of UHI mitigation for public health, energy efficiency, and environmental quality.
Critique the role of policy frameworks and citizen participation in fostering sustainable urban environments.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a city showing a mix of concrete buildings and green spaces, with heat maps overlay.
articles.trivia: The temperature difference between urban and rural areas can be as high as 10-12°C in extreme cases.

articles.headline: Lokpal and Lokayukta Act: Annual Report Highlights Progress and Challenges in Anti-Corruption Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The Lokpal of India presented its annual report, detailing the progress made in investigating corruption complaints against public functionaries and the challenges encountered.
• The report highlighted an increase in the number of cases processed, demonstrating the institution's growing functional capacity and outreach.
• However, it also pointed out delays in the appointment of Lokayuktas in several states and issues related to the timely submission of property declarations by public servants.
• Recommendations included strengthening the investigative wing of the Lokpal, ensuring financial and administrative autonomy, and greater public awareness campaigns about its mandate.
• The report underscores the ongoing efforts to enhance transparency and accountability in governance, while also emphasizing the need for robust institutional support.
gsSubTopics: Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Governance, transparency and accountability
Role of civil services in a democracy
prelimsPoints: Lokpal is an anti-corruption ombudsman for the Union government.
Lokayukta is the state-level equivalent.
Lokpal and Lokayukta Act was passed in 2013.
Jurisdiction includes Prime Minister (with caveats), Ministers, MPs, Group A, B, C, D officers.
Challenges include appointments and property declarations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and evolution of the Lokpal and Lokayukta institutions in India.
Analyze the achievements and inherent challenges in the functioning of the Lokpal and Lokayuktas.
Evaluate the effectiveness of these bodies in combating corruption and promoting good governance.
Examine the role of institutional independence and adequate resources for anti-corruption watchdogs.
Critique the legislative and administrative reforms needed to strengthen the Lokpal and Lokayukta framework.
articles.imageDescription: A balance scale with 'Transparency' on one side and 'Corruption' on the other, with a gavel nearby.
articles.trivia: The concept of an ombudsman originated in Sweden in 1809.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Achieve Breakthrough in Gene Editing for Inherited Rare Blood Disorders
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists at the National Institute of Biomedical Genomics (NIBMG) announced a significant breakthrough in gene editing technology for treating inherited rare blood disorders.
• The research focused on developing novel CRISPR-Cas9 based therapies to correct genetic mutations responsible for diseases like Thalassemia and Sickle Cell Anemia.
• The preclinical studies showed promising results in correcting disease-causing genes in patient-derived cells, paving the way for potential clinical trials.
• This advancement holds immense promise for millions suffering from rare genetic disorders, offering a curative approach rather than symptomatic treatment.
• Ethical considerations surrounding gene editing and the need for robust regulatory frameworks were also highlighted by the research team.
gsSubTopics: Biotechnology and issues relating to Intellectual Property Rights
Health
Science and Technology - developments and their applications
prelimsPoints: NIBMG (National Institute of Biomedical Genomics) is involved.
CRISPR-Cas9 is a gene-editing tool.
Targeted diseases: Thalassemia, Sickle Cell Anemia (inherited blood disorders).
Preclinical studies show promising results.
Raises ethical concerns in biotechnology.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of gene editing technologies like CRISPR-Cas9 in revolutionizing healthcare for genetic disorders.
Analyze the ethical, social, and regulatory challenges associated with human gene editing in India.
Evaluate the accessibility and affordability of advanced genetic therapies for the common population.
Examine the role of indigenous research in addressing India's specific health challenges and promoting self-reliance in biotechnology.
Critique the balance between scientific innovation and societal safeguards in emerging biotechnologies.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized DNA helix with glowing segments, representing gene editing at a molecular level.
articles.trivia: The Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2020 was awarded to Emmanuelle Charpentier and Jennifer A. Doudna for the development of a method for genome editing.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Scheme to Boost Traditional Indian Handicrafts and Artisan Livelihoods
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Textiles launched 'Hastkala Samvardhan Yojana', a new scheme aimed at revitalizing traditional Indian handicrafts and securing the livelihoods of artisans.
• The scheme provides financial assistance for raw material procurement, skill upgradation, design development, and market linkages both domestically and internationally.
• It focuses on promoting Geographical Indication (GI) tagged products and establishing e-commerce platforms to connect artisans directly with consumers.
• Special emphasis is placed on empowering women artisans and promoting sustainable practices in craft production.
• The initiative seeks to preserve India's rich cultural heritage, foster inclusive growth, and boost rural economies.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Social empowerment
prelimsPoints: Scheme name: 'Hastkala Samvardhan Yojana'.
Launched by Ministry of Textiles.
Focus on GI-tagged products.
Aims to empower women artisans.
Provides support for raw materials, skills, design, market linkage.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of traditional Indian handicrafts for cultural preservation and economic development.
Analyze the challenges faced by artisans in contemporary markets and the need for government intervention.
Evaluate the potential of schemes like 'Hastkala Samvardhan Yojana' in promoting inclusive growth and rural livelihoods.
Examine the role of GI tags and e-commerce platforms in enhancing the market access and value of handicrafts.
Critique the balance between modernizing production techniques and preserving traditional craftsmanship.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up of intricate Indian traditional handloom weaving, showcasing artisan skill.
articles.trivia: India has over 370 Geographical Indication (GI) tags, many of which are for traditional handicrafts and agricultural products.

articles.headline: Monsoon Progress Crucial for Kharif Crops: IMD Report on Agricultural Outlook and Food Security
articles.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) released its latest monsoon update, indicating crucial progress in rainfall distribution across key agricultural regions.
• While overall monsoon performance remains near normal, some regions have experienced deficits, raising concerns for Kharif crop sowing and yield.
• The report highlighted government preparedness with contingency plans for drought-prone areas and timely release of advisories to farmers.
• Focus remains on ensuring adequate reservoir levels and efficient water management to safeguard agricultural output and prevent food price inflation.
• The outlook for the remainder of the monsoon season will be critical for India's food security and the rural economy, which heavily relies on rain-fed agriculture.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena
Major crops cropping patterns
Food processing and related industries in India
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: Kharif crops are monsoon-dependent (e.g., rice, maize, jowar, bajra).
IMD is the primary agency for weather forecasting.
Monsoon performance is crucial for agricultural output.
Contingency plans for drought-prone areas.
El Niño and La Niña phenomena influence monsoons.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the monsoon in India's agriculture, economy, and food security.
Analyze the impact of uneven monsoon distribution and climate change on Kharif crop production and farmer livelihoods.
Evaluate the government's preparedness and contingency measures to address monsoon-related agricultural challenges.
Examine the strategies required for enhancing agricultural resilience to climate variability and ensuring sustainable food systems.
Critique the effectiveness of current agricultural policies in stabilizing food prices and supporting farmer incomes.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer standing in a lush green paddy field under cloudy skies, symbolizing monsoon agriculture.
articles.trivia: The Indian monsoon is driven by the differential heating of land and sea, a phenomenon known as the thermal theory.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Equitable Access to Vaccines and Medicines at Global Health Summit
articles.explainer:
• India's delegation at the Global Health Summit stressed the urgent need for a new international treaty on pandemic preparedness and equitable access to essential health products.
• The summit, attended by health ministers and experts worldwide, focused on strengthening global health architecture and addressing disparities revealed by recent health crises.
• India highlighted its role as a 'pharmacy of the world' and called for technology transfer mechanisms to boost local manufacturing capacities in developing nations.
• Discussions also covered intellectual property waivers for crucial medical technologies during health emergencies and collaborative research efforts.
• The initiative reflects India's commitment to global health solidarity and its leadership in advocating for the Global South's health interests.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora—their structure, mandate
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: India advocates for a new pandemic preparedness treaty.
Emphasizes equitable access to vaccines and medicines.
India known as 'pharmacy of the world'.
Discussions on IP waivers and technology transfer.
WHO is a key international health organization.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in global health diplomacy and its vision for a more equitable health architecture.
Analyze the challenges in ensuring universal and equitable access to essential health products during pandemics.
Evaluate the arguments for and against intellectual property waivers for medical technologies in health emergencies.
Examine the importance of technology transfer and local manufacturing in strengthening global health resilience.
Critique the current global health governance framework and suggest reforms for better pandemic preparedness.
articles.imageDescription: A globe surrounded by medical symbols and diverse hands, symbolizing global health cooperation.
articles.trivia: The World Health Organization (WHO) was established on 7 April 1948, celebrated as World Health Day.

articles.headline: Indian Scientists Discover New Species of Amphibian in Eastern Himalayas, Highlighting Biodiversity Richness
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian herpetologists announced the discovery of a new species of frog in the remote biodiversity hotspots of the Eastern Himalayas.
• The discovery underscores the immense, yet underexplored, biodiversity of the Himalayan region and its ecological fragility.
• Genetic analysis confirmed its distinctiveness, highlighting the unique evolutionary pressures in the region's diverse microhabitats.
• Researchers emphasized the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts in the Eastern Himalayas, which faces threats from climate change, habitat loss, and unregulated tourism.
• This finding contributes significantly to India's taxonomic knowledge and reinforces the importance of protecting pristine ecosystems.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: New species of frog discovered in Eastern Himalayas.
Eastern Himalayas is a biodiversity hotspot.
Discovery highlights ecological fragility and need for conservation.
Genetic analysis used for confirmation.
Amphibians are bio-indicators of environmental health.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of new species discoveries for understanding biodiversity and ecosystem health.
Analyze the unique ecological characteristics of the Eastern Himalayas and the threats it faces.
Evaluate the role of scientific research in informing conservation policies and practices.
Examine the challenges in exploring and protecting biodiversity in remote and inaccessible regions.
Critique the impact of human activities on fragile ecosystems and the importance of sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photo of a newly discovered frog species camouflaged in moss and leaves.
articles.trivia: The Eastern Himalayas are home to over 10,000 species of plants, 300 species of mammals, 977 species of birds, and 176 species of reptiles and amphibians.

articles.headline: RBI Report Highlights Surge in Digital Payments in Rural India, Driving Financial Inclusion
articles.explainer:
• A report by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) indicates a significant surge in digital payment adoption across rural and semi-urban areas, fueled by government initiatives.
• The growth is attributed to the expansion of the Unified Payments Interface (UPI) network, Jan Dhan accounts, and increasing mobile penetration.
• This trend is contributing significantly to financial inclusion, enabling easier access to financial services for previously unbanked populations.
• Challenges remain in terms of digital literacy, infrastructure reliability in remote areas, and addressing cybersecurity concerns among rural users.
• The RBI emphasized continued efforts to enhance digital payment infrastructure, promote financial education, and ensure robust security measures to sustain this growth.
gsSubTopics: Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government policies & interventions
prelimsPoints: RBI report on digital payment surge in rural India.
UPI and Jan Dhan accounts are key drivers.
Contributes to financial inclusion.
Challenges: digital literacy, infrastructure, cybersecurity.
NPCI operates UPI.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of digital payments in fostering financial inclusion and empowering rural economies in India.
Analyze the factors driving the adoption of digital payment technologies in rural and semi-urban areas.
Evaluate the challenges and risks associated with the rapid expansion of digital payments in less digitally literate populations.
Examine the government and RBI's initiatives to promote digital literacy and ensure secure digital transactions.
Critique the impact of digital financial services on poverty reduction and economic development in India.
articles.imageDescription: A rural Indian woman using a smartphone to make a digital payment at a local shop.
articles.trivia: India's UPI system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month for the first time in August 2023.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Launches Major Restoration Project for Ancient Temple Complex in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) initiated a large-scale restoration project for the ancient Ratnagiri Buddhist Monastery complex in Odisha, a significant heritage site.
• The project involves structural conservation, chemical preservation of sculptures, and landscaping to enhance visitor experience and protect the historical integrity of the site.
• Ratnagiri, part of the 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist sites in Odisha, holds immense archaeological and cultural importance, dating back to the 6th century CE.
• The initiative aims to promote heritage tourism, provide local employment, and educate the public about India's rich Buddhist legacy.
• Expert archaeologists and conservationists are collaborating to ensure that the restoration adheres to international standards of heritage preservation.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Important Geophysical phenomena (contextual for natural degradation effects)
prelimsPoints: Ratnagiri Buddhist Monastery is in Odisha.
Part of the 'Diamond Triangle' (Lalitgiri, Ratnagiri, Udayagiri).
Restoration by ASI.
Dates back to 6th century CE.
Focus on structural, chemical preservation and landscaping.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of heritage conservation for preserving India's cultural identity and promoting tourism.
Analyze the challenges involved in the restoration and maintenance of ancient archaeological sites.
Evaluate the role of the Archaeological Survey of India in protecting and promoting national heritage.
Examine the socio-economic benefits of heritage tourism for local communities.
Critique the balance between conservation efforts and development pressures around historical sites.
articles.imageDescription: Ruins of an ancient Buddhist stupa at Ratnagiri, with archaeologists working on site.
articles.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist sites in Odisha is known for its extensive monastic complexes and rich sculptural art.

highlights: India launches 'Green Hydrogen for Bharat' Phase II, targeting 10 MMTPA production by 2030 and $100 billion investment.
Supreme Court reinforces Right to Privacy, issuing stringent guidelines and mandating judicial oversight for digital surveillance.
India-EU Free Trade Agreement nearing conclusion, expected to boost bilateral trade by over 40% and open new market opportunities.
ISRO successfully conducts critical test flight for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV), aiming for low-cost space access and commercialization.
New National Cyber Security Framework 2.0 unveiled to safeguard critical infrastructure and bolster India's digital resilience against evolving threats.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Green Hydrogen for Bharat' Phase II, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to achieve a green hydrogen production capacity of 10 million metric tonnes per annum (MMTPA) by 2030.
2. It primarily focuses on reducing India's reliance on coal imports.
3. The Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition (SIGHT) Programme is a key component of this mission.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The mission explicitly targets 10 MMTPA green hydrogen production by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect. While green hydrogen helps reduce fossil fuel reliance, the primary focus is on fossil fuel imports in general, not specifically coal, and more broadly on energy security. Statement 3 is correct. The SIGHT Programme is indeed a key component, providing incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for establishing an independent oversight mechanism to review and approve surveillance requests, as per the recent Supreme Court ruling on the Right to Privacy?
options: (c) Judiciary, with an independent oversight mechanism
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Judiciary, with an independent oversight mechanism
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court ruling mandated the establishment of an independent oversight mechanism, possibly involving retired judges or technical experts, with judicial oversight as a mandatory component for reviewing and approving surveillance requests, reinforcing Article 21 and the Puttaswamy judgment principles.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Western Ghats:
1. It is one of the eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity in the world.
2. It is older than the Himalayan mountain range.
3. The recent IISc report highlighted habitat fragmentation as a major threat.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The Western Ghats are indeed one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity, are geologically older than the Himalayas, and the recent IISc report specifically identified habitat fragmentation, alongside other factors, as a major threat to its biodiversity.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Hastkala Samvardhan Yojana', recently launched by the Ministry of Textiles, primarily aims to:
options: (a) Promote textile exports through large-scale industrial production.
(b) Revitalize traditional Indian handicrafts and support artisan livelihoods.
(c) Establish new textile parks in underdeveloped regions.
(d) Provide subsidies for cotton farmers across the country.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Revitalize traditional Indian handicrafts and support artisan livelihoods.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Hastkala Samvardhan Yojana' is a new scheme specifically aimed at revitalizing traditional Indian handicrafts, providing financial assistance for various aspects like raw material procurement, skill upgradation, design development, and market linkages, directly supporting artisan livelihoods and cultural heritage.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following initiatives is NOT directly associated with the growth of digital payments and financial inclusion in rural India, as highlighted by the recent RBI report?
options: (d) Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cells
prelimsMocks.answer: (d) Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cells
prelimsMocks.explanation: The PLI Scheme for Advanced Chemistry Cells (ACC) is related to manufacturing and industrial growth, specifically for batteries, and is not directly associated with digital payments or financial inclusion in rural India. UPI, PMJDY, and mobile penetration are all key drivers of digital payment adoption and financial inclusion.

mainsMocks.question: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission is a critical step towards achieving energy security and climate goals. Discuss the multi-faceted implications of its Phase II for India's economy, environment, and geopolitical standing.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Environmental pollution & degradation; Government policies & interventions.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its objectives. Discuss its economic implications (investment, job creation, industrial growth, export potential). Analyze its environmental benefits (decarbonization, climate change mitigation). Evaluate its geopolitical impact (energy independence, global leadership in green tech). Conclude with challenges and the path forward.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent ruling on digital surveillance has significantly reinforced the Right to Privacy in India. Analyze the implications of this judgment for individual liberties and national security, and critically examine the challenges in implementing effective oversight mechanisms for state surveillance.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Fundamental Rights; Judiciary; Government policies & interventions; Internal security challenges.
mainsMocks.approach: Start with the context of Right to Privacy (Article 21, Puttaswamy judgment). Explain the core tenets of the new ruling (three-part test, judicial oversight). Discuss implications for individual liberties (protection from arbitrary surveillance, data protection). Analyze implications for national security (balancing security needs with privacy). Elaborate on challenges in implementing oversight (technical expertise, independence, accountability, political will). Conclude with a way forward for robust data governance.

mainsMocks.question: The implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 has shown mixed progress. Evaluate the successes and persistent challenges in its mid-term implementation, and suggest measures to ensure its transformative vision is fully realized.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources; Social empowerment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce NEP 2020's vision and key pillars. Discuss areas of success (e.g., foundational literacy, curriculum reforms, tech integration). Detail persistent challenges (e.g., GER targets, teacher shortages, infrastructure, equitable access). Suggest comprehensive measures for full realization (enhanced funding, teacher training, state-level collaboration, flexible implementation, digital infrastructure, community participation). Conclude on the long-term impact of a successfully implemented NEP.

quickBytes.text: India's target for green hydrogen production under Phase II is 10 MMTPA by 2030.
The Right to Privacy is enshrined under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution.
The Western Ghats are one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) aims to significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the national agency for cyber security incidents.
NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
The PLI Scheme for ACC batteries aims to reduce import dependence for electric vehicles.
The Lokpal and Lokayukta Act was passed in India in 2013.
CRISPR-Cas9 is a revolutionary gene-editing tool used in biotechnology.
Ratnagiri Buddhist Monastery, a heritage site, is located in Odisha.

GS1.headline: Conservationists Raise Alarm as New Report Highlights Threats to Western Ghats Biodiversity
GS1.explainer:
• A comprehensive report by the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) warns of escalating threats to the unique biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Key concerns include habitat fragmentation due to infrastructure projects, illegal mining, deforestation, and the increasing impact of climate change-induced extreme weather events.
• The report emphasizes the critical role of the Western Ghats as a global biodiversity hotspot and a source of numerous rivers, impacting the ecology and economy of peninsular India.
• It calls for stricter enforcement of environmental regulations, enhanced trans-state cooperation, and greater involvement of local communities in conservation efforts.
• Recommendations include establishing eco-sensitive zones around protected areas and promoting sustainable livelihoods to reduce human-wildlife conflict.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena
Environmental pollution & degradation
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Western Ghats are one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Report by IISc (Indian Institute of Science).
Major threats: habitat fragmentation, illegal mining, deforestation, climate change.
Home to numerous endemic species of flora and fauna.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces.
Analyze the socio-economic implications of environmental degradation in the region.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current conservation policies and suggest measures for improvement.
Examine the role of local communities and indigenous knowledge in biodiversity conservation.
Critique the balance between developmental projects and ecological preservation in ecologically sensitive areas.
GS1.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with a waterfall, symbolizing its rich biodiversity.
GS1.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and influence the Indian monsoon weather patterns.

GS1.headline: Mid-Term Review of NEP 2020 Implementation Reveals Mixed Progress and Key Challenges
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a mid-term review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, highlighting both achievements and persistent challenges.
• Progress has been noted in foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) initiatives, curriculum reforms, and the integration of technology in education.
• However, significant hurdles remain in achieving the stipulated Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) targets in higher education, addressing teacher shortages, and ensuring equitable access in remote areas.
• The report calls for accelerated efforts in teacher training, infrastructure development, and greater collaboration between central and state governments for effective policy rollout.
• Stakeholders emphasized the need for flexible implementation strategies tailored to regional specificities and robust funding mechanisms to realize NEP's transformative vision.
gsSubTopics: Development processes and the development industry
Social empowerment
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education.
Focus areas: FLN, curriculum flexibility, multidisciplinary approach.
Challenges: GER targets, teacher shortages, infrastructure.
NIPUN Bharat Mission is a key FLN initiative.
3-language formula is a part of NEP recommendations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformational potential of NEP 2020 for India's education system.
Discuss the key successes and challenges faced during the mid-term implementation of NEP 2020.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current strategies in addressing issues of equity, access, and quality in education.
Suggest policy interventions and resource mobilization strategies to overcome implementation hurdles.
Examine the role of technology and teacher training in achieving the goals of NEP 2020.
GS1.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom, with a teacher engaging them through digital tools, symbolizing education reform.
GS1.trivia: The Kothari Commission (1964-66) was instrumental in shaping India's education policy, advocating for a common school system.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs Launches National Strategy to Combat Urban Heat Island Effect
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs unveiled a comprehensive National Strategy to mitigate the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in major Indian cities.
• The strategy focuses on promoting green infrastructure, cool roofs, permeable pavements, and urban forestry to reduce ambient temperatures in densely populated areas.
• It mandates urban local bodies to integrate UHI mitigation measures into their master plans and building codes, with incentives for green building practices.
• Pilot projects will be initiated in 10 major cities to develop localized action plans and assess their effectiveness in reducing heat stress and energy consumption.
• The initiative aims to enhance urban resilience to climate change, improve public health outcomes, and reduce energy demand for cooling.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
Environmental pollution & degradation
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: UHI effect refers to warmer temperatures in urban areas compared to surrounding rural areas.
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched the strategy.
Measures include green roofs, cool pavements, urban forests.
Aims to reduce heat stress and energy consumption.
Pilot projects in 10 major cities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of the Urban Heat Island effect on Indian cities and their inhabitants.
Analyze the effectiveness of various green infrastructure and urban planning interventions in mitigating UHI.
Evaluate the challenges faced by urban local bodies in implementing sustainable and climate-resilient urban development strategies.
Examine the co-benefits of UHI mitigation for public health, energy efficiency, and environmental quality.
Critique the role of policy frameworks and citizen participation in fostering sustainable urban environments.
GS1.imageDescription: An aerial view of a city showing a mix of concrete buildings and green spaces, with heat maps overlay.
GS1.trivia: The temperature difference between urban and rural areas can be as high as 10-12°C in extreme cases.

GS1.headline: Government Announces New Scheme to Boost Traditional Indian Handicrafts and Artisan Livelihoods
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Textiles launched 'Hastkala Samvardhan Yojana', a new scheme aimed at revitalizing traditional Indian handicrafts and securing the livelihoods of artisans.
• The scheme provides financial assistance for raw material procurement, skill upgradation, design development, and market linkages both domestically and internationally.
• It focuses on promoting Geographical Indication (GI) tagged products and establishing e-commerce platforms to connect artisans directly with consumers.
• Special emphasis is placed on empowering women artisans and promoting sustainable practices in craft production.
• The initiative seeks to preserve India's rich cultural heritage, foster inclusive growth, and boost rural economies.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Social empowerment
prelimsPoints: Scheme name: 'Hastkala Samvardhan Yojana'.
Launched by Ministry of Textiles.
Focus on GI-tagged products.
Aims to empower women artisans.
Provides support for raw materials, skills, design, market linkage.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of traditional Indian handicrafts for cultural preservation and economic development.
Analyze the challenges faced by artisans in contemporary markets and the need for government intervention.
Evaluate the potential of schemes like 'Hastkala Samvardhan Yojana' in promoting inclusive growth and rural livelihoods.
Examine the role of GI tags and e-commerce platforms in enhancing the market access and value of handicrafts.
Critique the balance between modernizing production techniques and preserving traditional craftsmanship.
GS1.imageDescription: A close-up of intricate Indian traditional handloom weaving, showcasing artisan skill.
GS1.trivia: India has over 370 Geographical Indication (GI) tags, many of which are for traditional handicrafts and agricultural products.

GS1.headline: Monsoon Progress Crucial for Kharif Crops: IMD Report on Agricultural Outlook and Food Security
GS1.explainer:
• The India Meteorological Department (IMD) released its latest monsoon update, indicating crucial progress in rainfall distribution across key agricultural regions.
• While overall monsoon performance remains near normal, some regions have experienced deficits, raising concerns for Kharif crop sowing and yield.
• The report highlighted government preparedness with contingency plans for drought-prone areas and timely release of advisories to farmers.
• Focus remains on ensuring adequate reservoir levels and efficient water management to safeguard agricultural output and prevent food price inflation.
• The outlook for the remainder of the monsoon season will be critical for India's food security and the rural economy, which heavily relies on rain-fed agriculture.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena
Major crops cropping patterns
Food processing and related industries in India
Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
prelimsPoints: Kharif crops are monsoon-dependent (e.g., rice, maize, jowar, bajra).
IMD is the primary agency for weather forecasting.
Monsoon performance is crucial for agricultural output.
Contingency plans for drought-prone areas.
El Niño and La Niña phenomena influence monsoons.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of the monsoon in India's agriculture, economy, and food security.
Analyze the impact of uneven monsoon distribution and climate change on Kharif crop production and farmer livelihoods.
Evaluate the government's preparedness and contingency measures to address monsoon-related agricultural challenges.
Examine the strategies required for enhancing agricultural resilience to climate variability and ensuring sustainable food systems.
Critique the effectiveness of current agricultural policies in stabilizing food prices and supporting farmer incomes.
GS1.imageDescription: A farmer standing in a lush green paddy field under cloudy skies, symbolizing monsoon agriculture.
GS1.trivia: The Indian monsoon is driven by the differential heating of land and sea, a phenomenon known as the thermal theory.

• The project involves structural conservation, chemical preservation of sculptures, and landscaping to enhance visitor experience and protect the historical integrity of the site.
• Ratnagiri, part of the 'Diamond Triangle' of Buddhist sites in Odisha, holds immense archaeological and cultural importance, dating back to the 6th century CE.
• The initiative aims to promote heritage tourism, provide local employment, and educate the public about India's rich Buddhist legacy.
